market_note: Stablecoin supply has fallen $10B from its May peak as USDT and USDC contracted amid the broader market selloff, even as the GENIUS Act's July 18 rulemaking deadline passed with no federal agency issuing final rules.

stablecoin_items.headline: GENIUS Act Rulemaking Deadline Passes Without Final Rules
stablecoin_items.summary: The six federal agencies tasked with finalizing capital, reserve, and licensing rules for USDC and USAT issuers missed their July 18 deadline, with several still in public comment. Markets are treating it as a checkpoint, not a resolution.

stablecoin_items.headline: Stablecoin Supply Contracts $10B From May Peak
stablecoin_items.summary: USDT and USDC both shrank as the wider crypto market slid roughly 3% this cycle, though stablecoins have outperformed major Layer-1 tokens like ETH and SOL, which are down about 45%.

stablecoin_items.headline: Mastercard, Bank of America Push On-Chain Settlement
stablecoin_items.summary: Mastercard extended card settlement onto on-chain rails and Open Standard launched a 140-partner dollar stablecoin, while Bank of America teased instant cross-border real-time payments built on stablecoin rails.

stablecoin_items.headline: South Korea Launches First Government-Backed Stablecoin Pilot
stablecoin_items.summary: Gyeonggi Province confirmed an eight-month proof-of-concept for a government-backed blockchain stablecoin, set to begin in August 2026.

hack_items.headline: White Hats Find Aptos Flaw That Could've Risked $70B
hack_items.summary: Security firm Hexens discovered and helped patch a critical Aptos blockchain vulnerability with a near-90% exploit success rate and an attack cost of just hundreds of dollars, threatening stablecoins and cross-chain bridges.

hack_items.headline: Drift Protocol Hack Named Biggest of H1 2026 at $295M
hack_items.summary: The Solana-based DeFi exchange suffered the largest single crypto exploit of the first half of 2026, part of a record 207 hacking incidents industry-wide.

hack_items.headline: Infrastructure Compromises Drove 76% of H1 2026 Losses
hack_items.summary: Attacks on signing infrastructure and operational credentials caused the large majority of stolen funds despite accounting for only about 15% of all incidents, TRM Labs found.

hack_items.headline: North Korea Linked to 66% of H1 2026 Stolen Crypto Funds
hack_items.summary: Roughly $643 million of the nearly $972 million stolen across crypto platforms in H1 2026 is attributed to North Korea-linked hacking groups, per TRM Labs.
